text.en: Initiate kangaroo mother care; keep skin-to-skin under mother's clothing with cap and socks.
Encourage exclusive breastfeeding; if newborn cannot suckle, give expressed breast milk by spoon.
Refer to nearest SNCU / NBSU urgently; communicate findings on the HBNC card.

rules.rationale: MoHFW HBNC operational guidelines list seven neonatal danger signs; presence of any one mandates urgent referral with KMC en route.

text.en: Begin kangaroo mother care immediately; cover mother and baby together.
Move to a warm room; close windows; remove wet clothes.
Recheck axillary temperature in 30 minutes; refer if not improving or below 35 °C.

rules.rationale: Axillary temperature < 36.5 °C is hypothermia per HBNC; KMC restores warmth and reduces neonatal mortality.

text.en: Counsel and supervise daily KMC; weigh weekly.
Expressed breast milk by spoon if unable to suckle; do not bottle-feed.
Conduct all HBNC visits on schedule (Days 1, 3, 7, 14, 21, 28, 42); refer if any danger sign emerges.

rules.rationale: Weight < 2.5 kg is LBW per HBNC and requires intensified home-based care, frequent monitoring, and KMC.
